core.assert.orderPolicy: Evaluate the closed predicate tree in authored order.
core.assert.errorPolicy: Abort the complete candidate transaction when the predicate is false.

cost.formula: One step plus the bounded predicate tree.

counterexamples.reason: An assertion cannot omit its predicate.

core.entities.select.orderPolicy: Scan stable entity identifiers in canonical UTF-8 order.
core.entities.select.errorPolicy: Abort when the declared result cardinality cannot be met.

cost.formula: At most the declared collection capacity.

counterexamples.reason: Raw JSON paths are not selectors.

core.collection.id.allocate.inputType: Collection, typed sequence endpoint, and identifier prefix
core.collection.id.allocate.orderPolicy: Advance monotonically until the first collision-free identifier.
core.collection.id.allocate.errorPolicy: Abort on invalid or exhausted sequence state.

cost.formula: Bounded by collection capacity plus one sequence write.

counterexamples.reason: The sequence must be a typed StateRef.

core.sequence.next.inputType: Bounded unique sequence and current item
core.sequence.next.orderPolicy: Walk cyclically from the current item in authored sequence order.
core.sequence.next.errorPolicy: Abort when the sequence is invalid or has no eligible item.

cost.formula: At most the authored sequence length.

counterexamples.reason: An empty sequence has no deterministic successor.

core.state.patch.orderPolicy: Apply patches atomically in authored order.
core.state.patch.errorPolicy: Abort the transaction on the first incompatible patch.

cost.formula: One bounded write per patch.

counterexamples.reason: Raw state paths bypass typed endpoints.

core.number.add.errorPolicy: Abort on a non-numeric or non-finite result.

cost.formula: One endpoint read and one endpoint write.

counterexamples.reason: The target must be a StateRef, not a client player id.

core.resource.transfer.inputType: Bank, StateRef, or entity-field endpoints and non-negative amount
core.resource.transfer.orderPolicy: Debit before credit inside one candidate transaction.
core.resource.transfer.errorPolicy: Abort without partial writes when funds are insufficient.

cost.formula: At most two reads and two writes.

counterexamples.reason: Legacy player-metric endpoints are not admitted.

core.collection.append.inputType: Typed list StateRef and item expression
core.collection.append.orderPolicy: Append exactly once after the existing tail.
core.collection.append.errorPolicy: Abort when the endpoint is not a typed list.

cost.formula: Bounded by the declared list size.

counterexamples.reason: A map endpoint cannot be appended to.

core.entity.create.inputType: Typed entity collection and declared fields
core.entity.create.orderPolicy: Create one entity under its evaluated stable identifier.
core.entity.create.errorPolicy: Abort on duplicate id, capacity, type, or field mismatch.

cost.formula: One bounded entity write.

counterexamples.reason: Record-map collections are not entity constructors.

core.entity.facet.set.inputType: Entity reference, facet id, and typed value
core.entity.facet.set.errorPolicy: Abort when entity or facet is unavailable or read-only.

cost.formula: One entity lookup and one write.

counterexamples.reason: An attribute id cannot be written as a facet.

core.entity.attributes.patch.inputType: Entity reference and bounded attribute patches
core.entity.attributes.patch.orderPolicy: Apply nested patches in authored order.
core.entity.attributes.patch.errorPolicy: Abort on undeclared, read-only, or incompatible attributes.

cost.formula: One bounded write per authored patch.

counterexamples.reason: A patch path must start with a declared attribute field.

core.entities.update.inputType: Prior entity selection and declared field expressions
core.entities.update.orderPolicy: Update selected identifiers in canonical UTF-8 order.
core.entities.update.errorPolicy: Abort the complete transaction on any invalid entity or field.

cost.formula: Selection maximum multiplied by authored field count.

counterexamples.reason: The selection must be a preceding result.

core.event.emit.inputType: Declared event, summary, and typed payload
core.event.emit.orderPolicy: Preserve transaction step order in event and journal output.
core.event.emit.errorPolicy: Abort when payload or audience violates the event declaration.

cost.formula: Bounded by payload size and one optional journal append.

counterexamples.reason: An undeclared event type cannot be emitted.

core.entities.score.inputType: Record-map entities and bounded related entity sources
core.entities.score.orderPolicy: Scan related entities by canonical identifier and retain authored entity order.
core.entities.score.errorPolicy: Abort on unknown entities, invalid ownership, or unsafe integer aggregation.

cost.formula: Scored entity count multiplied by related collection capacities.

counterexamples.reason: The operation cannot name economic concepts such as coins.

core.ranking.stable.inputType: Neutral score result and bounded groups
core.ranking.stable.orderPolicy: Sort by descending score then canonical identifier; equal scores share rank.
core.ranking.stable.errorPolicy: Abort on unknown, duplicate, or malformed score entries.

cost.formula: Bounded group membership plus stable sort cost.

counterexamples.reason: Ranking does not write game state implicitly.

turn.phase.select.errorPolicy: Abort on unknown or non-public phase selection.

cost.formula: One platform turn-state write.

counterexamples.reason: Participant rotation is composed with core.sequence.next, not turn.advance.

random.dice.roll.inputType: Bounded dice notation, named stream, and numeric StateRef
random.dice.roll.orderPolicy: Advance only the named persisted stream.
random.dice.roll.errorPolicy: Abort on invalid dice, stream, or target type.

cost.formula: At most 99 bounded dice samples and one target write.

counterexamples.reason: An unnamed global random source is forbidden.

deck.shuffle.inputType: Source collection, deck id, and named stream
deck.shuffle.orderPolicy: Canonicalize source ids before Fisher-Yates shuffling.
deck.shuffle.errorPolicy: Abort on unknown collection, deck, or stream state.

cost.formula: Source collection capacity.

counterexamples.reason: A deck cannot expose its future order publicly.

deck.draw.inputType: Deck id, typed target, and empty policy
deck.draw.orderPolicy: Draw from the persisted top; reshuffle through the pinned stream only when declared.
deck.draw.errorPolicy: Abort or deterministically reshuffle according to onEmpty.

cost.formula: Constant unless bounded discard reshuffle is selected.

counterexamples.reason: An unknown empty policy is not executable.

graph.regions.route.plan.inputType: Network id and endpoint node expressions
graph.regions.route.plan.outputType: Pinned route geometry and segment count
graph.regions.route.plan.orderPolicy: Use the pinned region planner and deterministic tie stream.
graph.regions.route.plan.errorPolicy: Abort on unknown nodes, blocked route, or planner bounds.

cost.formula: Pinned region planner bounds plus edge collection capacity.

counterexamples.reason: Payment and lifecycle fields are game composition, not route input.

graph.edge.split.inputType: Network id, edge id, and normalized position
graph.edge.split.outputType: Created waypoint and replacement edge identifiers
graph.edge.split.orderPolicy: Replace one edge atomically with one node and two canonically identified edges.
graph.edge.split.errorPolicy: Abort on invalid position, capacity, ownership, or graph invariants.

cost.formula: Bounded collection scans and three entity writes.

counterexamples.reason: Waypoint cost and lifecycle are not graph invariants.

graph.entity.traverse.inputType: Network, movable entity, and traversed edge
graph.entity.traverse.orderPolicy: Move the primary entity then coupled entities in canonical order.
graph.entity.traverse.errorPolicy: Abort on disconnected traversal, ownership, or capacity violation.

cost.formula: Movement and capacity collection limits.

counterexamples.reason: Action-point spending is a separate resource operation.

graph.shortestPath.inputType: Network id and endpoint node expressions
graph.shortestPath.orderPolicy: Resolve equal paths by canonical node and edge identifier order.
graph.shortestPath.errorPolicy: Abort when no bounded path exists.

cost.formula: Declared node and edge collection capacities.

counterexamples.reason: Delivery payout is not a shortest-path responsibility.

relation.attach.inputType: Network, primary id, and bounded related ids
relation.attach.orderPolicy: Validate and attach related identifiers in canonical order.
relation.attach.errorPolicy: Abort atomically on ownership, co-location, compatibility, or capacity failure.

cost.formula: Related id count plus coupled collection capacity.

counterexamples.reason: Resource spending is composed separately.

relation.detach.inputType: Network, primary id, and bounded related ids
relation.detach.orderPolicy: Validate and detach related identifiers in canonical order.
relation.detach.errorPolicy: Abort atomically when a requested relation is absent or invalid.

cost.formula: Bounded related id count.

counterexamples.reason: A detach cannot name an empty related set.

system.schedule.register.inputType: Published system intent, schema-compatible parameters, and replay-safe trigger
system.schedule.register.orderPolicy: Register after the enclosing external transaction commits and evaluate the trigger against later committed state.
system.schedule.register.errorPolicy: Abort publication for external targets, incompatible parameters, actor context, or recursive system scheduling.

cost.formula: Bounded by sixteen scalar parameters and one depth-limited trigger.

counterexamples.reason: A scheduled intent must be explicitly marked for system invocation.

system.schedule.cancel.orderPolicy: Cancel the named pending schedule after the enclosing external transaction commits.
system.schedule.cancel.errorPolicy: Abort publication for non-string identifiers or recursive system scheduling.

cost.formula: One identifier evaluation and one schedule-ledger mutation.

counterexamples.reason: System intents cannot recursively mutate the schedule ledger.
